expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “Benchmark focus coverage”, the output explicitly states both parts of the case focus: (1) “Canonical top-layer grouping is required via the listed taxonomy … (Evidence: `skill_snapshot/references/phase4b-contract.md` → Canonical Top Layer)” and (2) “No scenario collapsing / preserve scenario granularity is explicitly required … ‘without merging away scenario granularity’ … ‘Anti-Compression Rule’ … (Evidence: `skill_snapshot/references/phase4b-contract.md`).”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4b
expectations.evidence: result.md declares “Phase under test: phase4b” and includes a dedicated section “Alignment with primary phase (phase4b)” describing Phase 4b entry/work/outputs/constraints/post-gates, concluding “This is directly aligned with the benchmark’s requested primary phase phase4b.” Execution notes likewise state “Phase model alignment: Phase 4b is responsible for ‘canonical top-layer grouping’.”
